content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/aragab/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/subagent-driven-development

# Subagent-Driven Development

Execute plan by dispatching fresh subagent per task, with two-stage review after each: spec compliance review first, then code quality review.

**Core principle:** Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec then quality) = high quality, fast iteration

## When to Use

```dot
digraph when_to_use {
    "Have implementation plan?" [shape=diamond];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" [shape=diamond];
    "Stay in this session?" [shape=diamond];
    "subagent-driven-development" [shape=box];
    "executing-plans" [shape=box];
    "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [shape=box];

    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Tasks mostly independent?" [label="yes"];
    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Stay in this session?" [label="yes"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no - tightly coupled"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "subagent-driven-development" [label="yes"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "executing-plans" [label="no - parallel session"];
}
```

**vs. Executing Plans (parallel session):**
- Same session (no context switch)
- Fresh subagent per task (no context pollution)
- Two-stage review after each task: spec compliance first, then code quality
- Faster iteration (no human-in-loop between tasks)

## The Process

```dot
digraph process {
    rankdir=TB;

    subgraph cluster_per_task {
        label="Per Task";
        "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent asks questions?" [shape=diamond];
        "Answer questions, provide context" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [shape=box];
        "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    }

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    "More tasks remain?" [shape=diamond];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [shape=box];
    "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch" [shape=box style=filled fillcolor=lightgreen];

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" -> "Implementer subagent asks questions?";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Answer questions, provide context" [label="yes"];
    "Answer questions, provide context" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?";
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?";
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [label="yes"];
    "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" -> "More tasks remain?";
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [label="no"];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" -> "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch";
}
```

## Prompt Templates

- `./implementer-prompt.md` - Dispatch implementer subagent
- `./spec-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch spec compliance reviewer subagent
- `./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent

## Example Workflow

```
You: I'm using Subagent-Driven Development to execute this plan.

[Read plan file once: docs/plans/feature-plan.md]
[Extract all 5 tasks with full text and context]
[Create TodoWrite with all tasks]

Task 1: Hook installation script

[Get Task 1 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: "Before I begin - should the hook be installed at user or system level?"

You: "User level (~/.config/superpowers/hooks/)"

Implementer: "Got it. Implementing now..."
[Later] Implementer:
  - Implemented install-hook command
  - Added tests, 5/5 passing
  - Self-review: Found I missed --force flag, added it
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant - all requirements met, nothing extra

[Get git SHAs, dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Good test coverage, clean. Issues: None. Approved.

[Mark Task 1 complete]

Task 2: Recovery modes

[Get Task 2 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: [No questions, proceeds]
Implementer:
  - Added verify/repair modes
  - 8/8 tests passing
  - Self-review: All good
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: ❌ Issues:
  - Missing: Progress reporting (spec says "report every 100 items")
  - Extra: Added --json flag (not requested)

[Implementer fixes issues]
Implementer: Removed --json flag, added progress reporting

[Spec reviewer reviews again]
Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant now

[Dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Solid. Issues (Important): Magic number (100)

[Implementer fixes]
Implementer: Extracted PROGRESS_INTERVAL constant

[Code reviewer reviews again]
Code reviewer: ✅ Approved

[Mark Task 2 complete]

...

[After all tasks]
[Dispatch final code-reviewer]
Final reviewer: All requirements met, ready to merge

Done!
```

## Advantages

**vs. Manual execution:**
- Subagents follow TDD naturally
- Fresh context per task (no confusion)
- Parallel-safe (subagents don't interfere)
- Subagent can ask questions (before AND during work)

**vs. Executing Plans:**
- Same session (no handoff)
- Continuous progress (no waiting)
- Review checkpoints automatic

**Efficiency gains:**
- No file reading overhead (controller provides full text)
- Controller curates exactly what context is needed
- Subagent gets complete information upfront
- Questions surfaced before work begins (not after)

**Quality gates:**
- Self-review catches issues before handoff
- Two-stage review: spec compliance, then code quality
- Review loops ensure fixes actually work
- Spec compliance prevents over/under-building
- Code quality ensures implementation is well-built

**Cost:**
- More subagent invocations (implementer + 2 reviewers per task)
- Controller does more prep work (extracting all tasks upfront)
- Review loops add iterations
- But catches issues early (cheaper than debugging later)

## Red Flags

**Never:**
- Start implementation on main/master branch without explicit user consent
- Skip reviews (spec compliance OR code quality)
- Proceed with unfixed issues
- Dispatch multiple implementation subagents in parallel (conflicts)
- Make subagent read plan file (provide full text instead)
- Skip scene-setting context (subagent needs to understand where task fits)
- Ignore subagent questions (answer before letting them proceed)
- Accept "close enough" on spec compliance (spec reviewer found issues = not done)
- Skip review loops (reviewer found issues = implementer fixes = review again)
- Let implementer self-review replace actual review (both are needed)
- **Start code quality review before spec compliance is ✅** (wrong order)
- Move to next task while either review has open issues

**If subagent asks questions:**
- Answer clearly and completely
- Provide additional context if needed
- Don't rush them into implementation

**If reviewer finds issues:**
- Implementer (same subagent) fixes them
- Reviewer reviews again
- Repeat until approved
- Don't skip the re-review

**If subagent fails task:**
- Dispatch fix subagent with specific instructions
- Don't try to fix manually (context pollution)

## Integration

**Required workflow skills:**
- **superpowers:using-git-worktrees** - REQUIRED: Set up isolated workspace before starting
- **superpowers:writing-plans** - Creates the plan this skill executes
- **superpowers:requesting-code-review** - Code review template for reviewer subagents
- **superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch** - Complete development after all tasks

**Subagents should use:**
- **superpowers:test-driven-development** - Subagents follow TDD for each task

**Alternative workflow:**
- **superpowers:executing-plans** - Use for parallel session instead of same-session execution

input.description: Implement HTTP evaluator server
input.prompt:
You are implementing Task 1 of a plan. Your job is to create a file, test it works, and commit.

## Context

The project `optimize-anything` is a CLI tool for optimizing text artifacts. It has evaluators that score candidates. The evaluator protocol is: stdin receives `{"candidate": "..."}`, stdout produces `{"score": 0.85, ...}`.

There's an existing command evaluator at `evaluators/skill_clarity.sh` that follows this protocol.

The CLI already supports `--evaluator-url` which sends POST requests with `{"candidate": "..."}` to an HTTP endpoint and expects JSON back with a `score` field.

## Your Task

Create `scripts/evaluator_http_server.py` — a minimal stdlib HTTP server that wraps any command evaluator behind HTTP.

### Requirements

1. **POST /** — accepts JSON `{"candidate": "..."}`, pipes it to the command evaluator via stdin as `{"candidate": "..."}`, returns the evaluator's JSON stdout response
2. **GET /** — returns `{"status": "ok"}` for health/preflight checks  
3. **CLI args**: `--port` (default 8234), `--evaluator-command` (nargs="+", required, must be last arg)
4. **Error handling**: subprocess failures return `{"score": 0.0, "error": "..."}` with HTTP 200 (matches evaluator protocol)
5. **Startup signal**: prints `Listening on http://localhost:{port}` to stderr so callers know it's ready
6. **Bind to localhost only** for security
7. **No external dependencies** — stdlib only (`http.server`, `subprocess`, `argparse`, `json`)
8. **Compact logging** to stderr with `[evaluator-http]` prefix
9. **Clean shutdown** on KeyboardInterrupt/SIGINT

### Implementation

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Thin HTTP server wrapping a command evaluator.

Accepts POST / with {"candidate": "..."}, pipes to the command evaluator
via stdin, and returns the evaluator's JSON response.

GET / returns {"status": "ok"} for health/preflight checks.

Usage:
    python scripts/evaluator_http_server.py --evaluator-command bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh
    python scripts/evaluator_http_server.py --port 9000 --evaluator-command bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import subprocess
import sys
from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler


class EvaluatorHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    """HTTP handler that delegates scoring to a command evaluator."""

    evaluator_command: list[str] = []

    def do_GET(self) -> None:
        """Health check endpoint."""
        self._send_json(200, {"status": "ok"})

    def do_POST(self) -> None:
        """Score a candidate via the command evaluator."""
        content_length = int(self.headers.get("Content-Length", 0))
        if content_length == 0:
            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Empty request body"})
            return

        body = self.rfile.read(content_length)
        try:
            payload = json.loads(body)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Invalid JSON in request body"})
            return

        if "candidate" not in payload:
            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Missing 'candidate' field"})
            return

        # Forward to command evaluator
        stdin_data = json.dumps({"candidate": payload["candidate"]})
        try:
            proc = subprocess.run(
                self.evaluator_command,
                input=stdin_data,
                capture_output=True,
                text=True,
                timeout=60,
            )
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Evaluator command timed out"})
            return
        except FileNotFoundError:
            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": f"Command not found: {self.evaluator_command[0]}"})
            return
        except OSError as e:
            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": f"Command failed: {e}"})
            return

        if proc.returncode != 0:
            self._send_json(200, {
                "score": 0.0,
                "error": f"Evaluator exited with code {proc.returncode}",
                "stderr": proc.stderr.strip()[:500],
            })
            return

        # Parse and forward the evaluator's JSON response
        try:
            result = json.loads(proc.stdout)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            self._send_json(200, {
                "score": 0.0,
                "error": "Evaluator output is not valid JSON",
                "stdout": proc.stdout.strip()[:500],
            })
            return

        self._send_json(200, result)

    def _send_json(self, status: int, data: dict) -> None:
        response = json.dumps(data)
        self.send_response(status)
        self.send_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
        self.send_header("Content-Length", str(len(response)))
        self.end_headers()
        self.wfile.write(response.encode())

    def log_message(self, format: str, *args) -> None:
        """Log to stderr with compact format."""
        print(f"[evaluator-http] {args[0]}", file=sys.stderr)


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="evaluator-http-server",
        description="HTTP server wrapping a command evaluator",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8234, help="Port to listen on")
    # Must be last due to nargs="+"
    parser.add_argument(
        "--evaluator-command",
        nargs="+",
        required=True,
        help="Command evaluator to wrap (e.g., bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh)",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    EvaluatorHandler.evaluator_command = args.evaluator_command

    server = HTTPServer(("localhost", args.port), EvaluatorHandler)
    print(f"Listening on http://localhost:{args.port}", file=sys.stderr)
    try:
        server.serve_forever()
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        print("\nShutting down.", file=sys.stderr)
    server.server_close()
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
```

### After writing the file

1. Write the file to `scripts/evaluator_http_server.py`
2. Run the existing test suite to verify nothing is broken: `uv run pytest --tb=short -q`
3. Commit: `git add scripts/evaluator_http_server.py && git commit -m "feat: add HTTP evaluator server wrapping command evaluators"`

Do NOT modify any other files. Do NOT add tests for this file (testing happens live in Task 3).

### Requirements

1. **POST /** — accepts JSON `{"candidate": "..."}`, pipes it to the command evaluator via stdin as `{"candidate": "..."}`, returns the evaluator's JSON stdout response
2. **GET /** — returns `{"status": "ok"}` for health/preflight checks

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Thin HTTP server wrapping a command evaluator.

Accepts POST / with {"candidate": "..."}, pipes to the command evaluator
via stdin, and returns the evaluator's JSON response.

GET / returns {"status": "ok"} for health/preflight checks.

Usage:
    python scripts/evaluator_http_server.py --evaluator-command bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh
    python scripts/evaluator_http_server.py --port 9000 --evaluator-command bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import subprocess
import sys
from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler


class EvaluatorHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    """HTTP handler that delegates scoring to a command evaluator."""

    evaluator_command: list[str] = []

    def do_GET(self) -> None:
        """Health check endpoint."""
        self._send_json(200, {"status": "ok"})

    def do_POST(self) -> None:
        """Score a candidate via the command evaluator."""
        content_length = int(self.headers.get("Content-Length", 0))
        if content_length == 0:
            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Empty request body"})
            return

        body = self.rfile.read(content_length)
        try:
            payload = json.loads(body)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Invalid JSON in request body"})
            return

        if "candidate" not in payload:
            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Missing 'candidate' field"})
            return

        # Forward to command evaluator
        stdin_data = json.dumps({"candidate": payload["candidate"]})
        try:
            proc = subprocess.run(
                self.evaluator_command,
                input=stdin_data,
                capture_output=True,
                text=True,
                timeout=60,
            )
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Evaluator command timed out"})
            return
        except FileNotFoundError:
            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": f"Command not found: {self.evaluator_command[0]}"})
            return
        except OSError as e:
            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": f"Command failed: {e}"})
            return

        if proc.returncode != 0:
            self._send_json(200, {
                "score": 0.0,
                "error": f"Evaluator exited with code {proc.returncode}",
                "stderr": proc.stderr.strip()[:500],
            })
            return

        # Parse and forward the evaluator's JSON response
        try:
            result = json.loads(proc.stdout)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            self._send_json(200, {
                "score": 0.0,
                "error": "Evaluator output is not valid JSON",
                "stdout": proc.stdout.strip()[:500],
            })
            return

        self._send_json(200, result)

    def _send_json(self, status: int, data: dict) -> None:
        response = json.dumps(data)
        self.send_response(status)
        self.send_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
        self.send_header("Content-Length", str(len(response)))
        self.end_headers()
        self.wfile.write(response.encode())

    def log_message(self, format: str, *args) -> None:
        """Log to stderr with compact format."""
        print(f"[evaluator-http] {args[0]}", file=sys.stderr)


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="evaluator-http-server",
        description="HTTP server wrapping a command evaluator",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8234, help="Port to listen on")
    # Must be last due to nargs="+"
    parser.add_argument(
        "--evaluator-command",
        nargs="+",
        required=True,
        help="Command evaluator to wrap (e.g., bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh)",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    EvaluatorHandler.evaluator_command = args.evaluator_command

    server = HTTPServer(("localhost", args.port), EvaluatorHandler)
    print(f"Listening on http://localhost:{args.port}", file=sys.stderr)
    try:
        server.serve_forever()
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        print("\nShutting down.", file=sys.stderr)
    server.server_close()
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
```

### After writing the file

1. Write the file to `scripts/evaluator_http_server.py`
2. Run the existing test suite to verify nothing is broken: `uv run pytest --tb=short -q`
3. Commit: `git add scripts/evaluator_http_server.py && git commit -m "feat: add HTTP evaluator server wrapping command evaluators"`

Do NOT modify any other files. Do NOT add tests for this file (testing happens live in Task 3).

## Context

The project `optimize-anything` is a CLI tool for optimizing text artifacts. It has evaluators that score candidates. The evaluator protocol is: stdin receives `{"candidate": "..."}`, stdout produces `{"score": 0.85, ...}`.

There's an existing command evaluator at `evaluators/skill_clarity.sh` that follows this protocol.

The CLI already supports `--evaluator-url` which sends POST requests with `{"candidate": "..."}` to an HTTP endpoint and expects JSON back with a `score` field.

## Your Task

Create `scripts/evaluator_http_server.py` — a minimal stdlib HTTP server that wraps any command evaluator behind HTTP.

8. **Compact logging** to stderr with `[evaluator-http]` prefix
9. **Clean shutdown** on KeyboardInterrupt/SIGINT

```python
import json
import subprocess
import sys
from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler


class EvaluatorHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    """HTTP handler that delegates scoring to a command evaluator."""

    evaluator_command: list[str] = []

    def do_GET(self) -> None:
        """Health check endpoint."""
        self._send_json(200, {"status": "ok"})

    def do_POST(self) -> None:
        """Score a candidate via the command evaluator."""
        content_length = int(self.headers.get("Content-Length", 0))
        if content_length == 0:
            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Empty request body"})
            return

        body = self.rfile.read(content_length)
        try:
            payload = json.loads(body)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Invalid JSON in request body"})
            return

        if "candidate" not in payload:
            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Missing 'candidate' field"})
            return

        # Forward to command evaluator
        stdin_data = json.dumps({"candidate": payload["candidate"]})
        try:
            proc = subprocess.run(
                self.evaluator_command,
                input=stdin_data,
                capture_output=True,
                text=True,
                timeout=60,
            )
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Evaluator command timed out"})
            return
        except FileNotFoundError:
            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": f"Command not found: {self.evaluator_command[0]}"})
            return
        except OSError as e:
            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": f"Command failed: {e}"})
            return

        if proc.returncode != 0:
            self._send_json(200, {
                "score": 0.0,
                "error": f"Evaluator exited with code {proc.returncode}",
                "stderr": proc.stderr.strip()[:500],
            })
            return

        # Parse and forward the evaluator's JSON response
        try:
            result = json.loads(proc.stdout)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            self._send_json(200, {
                "score": 0.0,
                "error": "Evaluator output is not valid JSON",
                "stdout": proc.stdout.strip()[:500],
            })
            return

        self._send_json(200, result)

    def _send_json(self, status: int, data: dict) -> None:
        response = json.dumps(data)
        self.send_response(status)
        self.send_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
        self.send_header("Content-Length", str(len(response)))
        self.end_headers()
        self.wfile.write(response.encode())

    def log_message(self, format: str, *args) -> None:
        """Log to stderr with compact format."""
        print(f"[evaluator-http] {args[0]}", file=sys.stderr)


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="evaluator-http-server",
        description="HTTP server wrapping a command evaluator",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8234, help="Port to listen on")
    # Must be last due to nargs="+"
    parser.add_argument(
        "--evaluator-command",
        nargs="+",
        required=True,
        help="Command evaluator to wrap (e.g., bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh)",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    EvaluatorHandler.evaluator_command = args.evaluator_command

    server = HTTPServer(("localhost", args.port), EvaluatorHandler)
    print(f"Listening on http://localhost:{args.port}", file=sys.stderr)
    try:
        server.serve_forever()
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        print("\nShutting down.", file=sys.stderr)
    server.server_close()
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
```

### After writing the file

1. Write the file to `scripts/evaluator_http_server.py`
2. Run the existing test suite to verify nothing is broken: `uv run pytest --tb=short -q`
3. Commit: `git add scripts/evaluator_http_server.py && git commit -m "feat: add HTTP evaluator server wrapping command evaluators"`

Do NOT modify any other files. Do NOT add tests for this file (testing happens live in Task 3).

data.prompt:
You are implementing Task 1 of a plan. Your job is to create a file, test it works, and commit.

## Context

The project `optimize-anything` is a CLI tool for optimizing text artifacts. It has evaluators that score candidates. The evaluator protocol is: stdin receives `{"candidate": "..."}`, stdout produces `{"score": 0.85, ...}`.

There's an existing command evaluator at `evaluators/skill_clarity.sh` that follows this protocol.

The CLI already supports `--evaluator-url` which sends POST requests with `{"candidate": "..."}` to an HTTP endpoint and expects JSON back with a `score` field.

## Your Task

Create `scripts/evaluator_http_server.py` — a minimal stdlib HTTP server that wraps any command evaluator behind HTTP.

### Requirements

1. **POST /** — accepts JSON `{"candidate": "..."}`, pipes it to the command evaluator via stdin as `{"candidate": "..."}`, returns the evaluator's JSON stdout response
2. **GET /** — returns `{"status": "ok"}` for health/preflight checks  
3. **CLI args**: `--port` (default 8234), `--evaluator-command` (nargs="+", required, must be last arg)
4. **Error handling**: subprocess failures return `{"score": 0.0, "error": "..."}` with HTTP 200 (matches evaluator protocol)
5. **Startup signal**: prints `Listening on http://localhost:{port}` to stderr so callers know it's ready
6. **Bind to localhost only** for security
7. **No external dependencies** — stdlib only (`http.server`, `subprocess`, `argparse`, `json`)
8. **Compact logging** to stderr with `[evaluator-http]` prefix
9. **Clean shutdown** on KeyboardInterrupt/SIGINT

### Implementation

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Thin HTTP server wrapping a command evaluator.

Accepts POST / with {"candidate": "..."}, pipes to the command evaluator
via stdin, and returns the evaluator's JSON response.

GET / returns {"status": "ok"} for health/preflight checks.

Usage:
    python scripts/evaluator_http_server.py --evaluator-command bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh
    python scripts/evaluator_http_server.py --port 9000 --evaluator-command bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import subprocess
import sys
from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler


class EvaluatorHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    """HTTP handler that delegates scoring to a command evaluator."""

    evaluator_command: list[str] = []

    def do_GET(self) -> None:
        """Health check endpoint."""
        self._send_json(200, {"status": "ok"})

    def do_POST(self) -> None:
        """Score a candidate via the command evaluator."""
        content_length = int(self.headers.get("Content-Length", 0))
        if content_length == 0:
            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Empty request body"})
            return

        body = self.rfile.read(content_length)
        try:
            payload = json.loads(body)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Invalid JSON in request body"})
            return

        if "candidate" not in payload:

        self._send_json(200, result)

    def _send_json(self, status: int, data: dict) -> None:
        response = json.dumps(data)
        self.send_response(status)
        self.send_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
        self.send_header("Content-Length", str(len(response)))
        self.end_headers()
        self.wfile.write(response.encode())

    def log_message(self, format: str, *args) -> None:
        """Log to stderr with compact format."""
        print(f"[evaluator-http] {args[0]}", file=sys.stderr)


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
```

You are implementing Task 1 of a plan. Your job is to create a file, test it works, and commit.

## Context

The project `optimize-anything` is a CLI tool for optimizing text artifacts. It has evaluators that score candidates. The evaluator protocol is: stdin receives `{"candidate": "..."}`, stdout produces `{"score": 0.85, ...}`.

There's an existing command evaluator at `evaluators/skill_clarity.sh` that follows this protocol.

The CLI already supports `--evaluator-url` which sends POST requests with `{"candidate": "..."}` to an HTTP endpoint and expects JSON back with a `score` field.

## Your Task

Create `scripts/evaluator_http_server.py` — a minimal stdlib HTTP server that wraps any command evaluator behind HTTP.

### Requirements

1. **POST /** — accepts JSON `{"candidate": "..."}`, pipes it to the command evaluator via stdin as `{"candidate": "..."}`, returns the evaluator's JSON stdout response
2. **GET /** — returns `{"status": "ok"}` for health/preflight checks  
3. **CLI args**: `--port` (default 8234), `--evaluator-command` (nargs="+", required, must be last arg)
4. **Error handling**: subprocess failures return `{"score": 0.0, "error": "..."}` with HTTP 200 (matches evaluator protocol)
5. **Startup signal**: prints `Listening on http://localhost:{port}` to stderr so callers know it's ready
6. **Bind to localhost only** for security
7. **No external dependencies** — stdlib only (`http.server`, `subprocess`, `argparse`, `json`)
8. **Compact logging** to stderr with `[evaluator-http]` prefix
9. **Clean shutdown** on KeyboardInterrupt/SIGINT

### Implementation

You are implementing Task 1 of a plan. Your job is to create a file, test it works, and commit.

## Context

The project `optimize-anything` is a CLI tool for optimizing text artifacts. It has evaluators that score candidates. The evaluator protocol is: stdin receives `{"candidate": "..."}`, stdout produces `{"score": 0.85, ...}`.

There's an existing command evaluator at `evaluators/skill_clarity.sh` that follows this protocol.

The CLI already supports `--evaluator-url` which sends POST requests with `{"candidate": "..."}` to an HTTP endpoint and expects JSON back with a `score` field.

## Your Task

Create `scripts/evaluator_http_server.py` — a minimal stdlib HTTP server that wraps any command evaluator behind HTTP.

### Requirements

1. **POST /** — accepts JSON `{"candidate": "..."}`, pipes it to the command evaluator via stdin as `{"candidate": "..."}`, returns the evaluator's JSON stdout response
2. **GET /** — returns `{"status": "ok"}` for health/preflight checks  
3. **CLI args**: `--port` (default 8234), `--evaluator-command` (nargs="+", required, must be last arg)
4. **Error handling**: subprocess failures return `{"score": 0.0, "error": "..."}` with HTTP 200 (matches evaluator protocol)
5. **Startup signal**: prints `Listening on http://localhost:{port}` to stderr so callers know it's ready
6. **Bind to localhost only** for security
7. **No external dependencies** — stdlib only (`http.server`, `subprocess`, `argparse`, `json`)
8. **Compact logging** to stderr with `[evaluator-http]` prefix
9. **Clean shutdown** on KeyboardInterrupt/SIGINT

### Implementation

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Thin HTTP server wrapping a command evaluator.

Accepts POST / with {"candidate": "..."}, pipes to the command evaluator
via stdin, and returns the evaluator's JSON response.

GET / returns {"status": "ok"} for health/preflight checks.

Usage:
    python scripts/evaluator_http_server.py --evaluator-command bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh
    python scripts/evaluator_http_server.py --port 9000 --evaluator-command bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import subprocess
import sys
from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler


class EvaluatorHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    """HTTP handler that delegates scoring to a command evaluator."""

    evaluator_command: list[str] = []

    def do_GET(self) -> None:
        """Health check endpoint."""
        self._send_json(200, {"status": "ok"})

    def do_POST(self) -> None:
        """Score a candidate via the command evaluator."""
        content_length = int(self.headers.get("Content-Length", 0))
        if content_length == 0:
            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Empty request body"})
            return

        body = self.rfile.read(content_length)
        try:
            payload = json.loads(body)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Invalid JSON in request body"})
            return

        if "candidate" not in payload:
            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Missing 'candidate' field"})
            return

        # Forward to command evaluator
        stdin_data = json.dumps({"candidate": payload["candidate"]})
        try:
            proc = subprocess.run(
                self.evaluator_command,
                input=stdin_data,
                capture_output=True,
                text=True,
                timeout=60,
            )
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Evaluator command timed out"})
            return
        except FileNotFoundError:
            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": f"Command not found: {self.evaluator_command[0]}"})
            return
        except OSError as e:
            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": f"Command failed: {e}"})
            return

        if proc.returncode != 0:
            self._send_json(200, {
                "score": 0.0,
                "error": f"Evaluator exited with code {proc.returncode}",
                "stderr": proc.stderr.strip()[:500],
            })
            return

        # Parse and forward the evaluator's JSON response
        try:
            result = json.loads(proc.stdout)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            self._send_json(200, {
                "score": 0.0,
                "error": "Evaluator output is not valid JSON",
                "stdout": proc.stdout.strip()[:500],
            })
            return

        self._send_json(200, result)

    def _send_json(self, status: int, data: dict) -> None:
        response = json.dumps(data)
        self.send_response(status)
        self.send_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
        self.send_header("Content-Length", str(len(response)))
        self.end_headers()
        self.wfile.write(response.encode())

    def log_message(self, format: str, *args) -> None:
        """Log to stderr with compact format."""
        print(f"[evaluator-http] {args[0]}", file=sys.stderr)


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="evaluator-http-server",
        description="HTTP server wrapping a command evaluator",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8234, help="Port to listen on")
    # Must be last due to nargs="+"
    parser.add_argument(
        "--evaluator-command",
        nargs="+",
        required=True,
        help="Command evaluator to wrap (e.g., bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh)",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    EvaluatorHandler.evaluator_command = args.evaluator_command

    server = HTTPServer(("localhost", args.port), EvaluatorHandler)
    print(f"Listening on http://localhost:{args.port}", file=sys.stderr)
    try:
        server.serve_forever()
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        print("\nShutting down.", file=sys.stderr)
    server.server_close()
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
```

data.prompt:
You are implementing Task 1 of a plan. Your job is to create a file, test it works, and commit.

## Context

The project `optimize-anything` is a CLI tool for optimizing text artifacts. It has evaluators that score candidates. The evaluator protocol is: stdin receives `{"candidate": "..."}`, stdout produces `{"score": 0.85, ...}`.

There's an existing command evaluator at `evaluators/skill_clarity.sh` that follows this protocol.

The CLI already supports `--evaluator-url` which sends POST requests with `{"candidate": "..."}` to an HTTP endpoint and expects JSON back with a `score` field.

## Your Task

Create `scripts/evaluator_http_server.py` — a minimal stdlib HTTP server that wraps any command evaluator behind HTTP.

### Requirements

1. **POST /** — accepts JSON `{"candidate": "..."}`, pipes it to the command evaluator via stdin as `{"candidate": "..."}`, returns the evaluator's JSON stdout response
2. **GET /** — returns `{"status": "ok"}` for health/preflight checks  
3. **CLI args**: `--port` (default 8234), `--evaluator-command` (nargs="+", required, must be last arg)
4. **Error handling**: subprocess failures return `{"score": 0.0, "error": "..."}` with HTTP 200 (matches evaluator protocol)
5. **Startup signal**: prints `Listening on http://localhost:{port}` to stderr so callers know it's ready
6. **Bind to localhost only** for security
7. **No external dependencies** — stdlib only (`http.server`, `subprocess`, `argparse`, `json`)
8. **Compact logging** to stderr with `[evaluator-http]` prefix
9. **Clean shutdown** on KeyboardInterrupt/SIGINT

### Implementation

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Thin HTTP server wrapping a command evaluator.

Accepts POST / with {"candidate": "..."}, pipes to the command evaluator
via stdin, and returns the evaluator's JSON response.

GET / returns {"status": "ok"} for health/preflight checks.

Usage:
    python scripts/evaluator_http_server.py --evaluator-command bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh
    python scripts/evaluator_http_server.py --port 9000 --evaluator-command bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import subprocess
import sys
from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler


class EvaluatorHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    """HTTP handler that delegates scoring to a command evaluator."""

    evaluator_command: list[str] = []

    def do_GET(self) -> None:
        """Health check endpoint."""
        self._send_json(200, {"status": "ok"})

    def do_POST(self) -> None:
        """Score a candidate via the command evaluator."""
        content_length = int(self.headers.get("Content-Length", 0))
        if content_length == 0:
            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Empty request body"})
            return

        body = self.rfile.read(content_length)
        try:
            payload = json.loads(body)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Invalid JSON in request body"})
            return

        if "candidate" not in payload:
            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Missing 'candidate' field"})
            return

        # Forward to command evaluator
        stdin_data = json.dumps({"candidate": payload["candidate"]})
        try:
            proc = subprocess.run(
                self.evaluator_command,
                input=stdin_data,
                capture_output=True,
                text=True,
                timeout=60,
            )
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Evaluator command timed out"})
            return
        except FileNotFoundError:
            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": f"Command not found: {self.evaluator_command[0]}"})
            return
        except OSError as e:
            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": f"Command failed: {e}"})
            return

        if proc.returncode != 0:
            self._send_json(200, {
                "score": 0.0,
                "error": f"Evaluator exited with code {proc.returncode}",
                "stderr": proc.stderr.strip()[:500],
            })
            return

        # Parse and forward the evaluator's JSON response
        try:
            result = json.loads(proc.stdout)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            self._send_json(200, {
                "score": 0.0,
                "error": "Evaluator output is not valid JSON",
                "stdout": proc.stdout.strip()[:500],
            })
            return

        self._send_json(200, result)

    def _send_json(self, status: int, data: dict) -> None:
        response = json.dumps(data)
        self.send_response(status)
        self.send_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
        self.send_header("Content-Length", str(len(response)))
        self.end_headers()
        self.wfile.write(response.encode())

    def log_message(self, format: str, *args) -> None:
        """Log to stderr with compact format."""
        print(f"[evaluator-http] {args[0]}", file=sys.stderr)


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="evaluator-http-server",
        description="HTTP server wrapping a command evaluator",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8234, help="Port to listen on")
    # Must be last due to nargs="+"
    parser.add_argument(
        "--evaluator-command",
        nargs="+",
        required=True,
        help="Command evaluator to wrap (e.g., bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh)",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    EvaluatorHandler.evaluator_command = args.evaluator_command

    server = HTTPServer(("localhost", args.port), EvaluatorHandler)
    print(f"Listening on http://localhost:{args.port}", file=sys.stderr)
    try:
        server.serve_forever()
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        print("\nShutting down.", file=sys.stderr)
    server.server_close()
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
```

2. Run the existing test suite to verify nothing is broken: `uv run pytest --tb=short -q`
3. Commit: `git add scripts/evaluator_http_server.py && git commit -m "feat: add HTTP evaluator server wrapping command evaluators"`

**What it does:** A minimal stdlib-only HTTP server that wraps any command evaluator behind an HTTP endpoint. This bridges the gap between `--evaluator-command` scripts (like `evaluators/skill_clarity.sh`) and the CLI's `--evaluator-url` flag.

**Key features:**
- **POST /** — accepts `{"candidate": "..."}` JSON, pipes it to the command evaluator via stdin, returns the evaluator's JSON stdout response

- **CLI args:** `--port` (default 8234) and `--evaluator-command` (nargs="+", required, must be last arg)
- **Error handling:** subprocess failures (timeout, not found, non-zero exit, invalid JSON output) all return `{"score": 0.0, "error": "..."}` with HTTP 200, matching the evaluator protocol
- **Startup signal:** prints `Listening on http://localhost:{port}` to stderr
- **Security:** binds to localhost only
- **No external dependencies:** uses only `http.server`, `subprocess`, `argparse`, `json`
- **Compact logging:** `[evaluator-http]` prefix on stderr
- **Clean shutdown:** handles `KeyboardInterrupt`

**Verification:**
- All 222 existing tests pass
- Script imports and parses cleanly
- Committed as `4b6864d`

```python
            return

        if proc.returncode != 0:
            self._send_json(200, {
                "score": 0.0,
                "error": f"Evaluator exited with code {proc.returncode}",
                "stderr": proc.stderr.strip()[:500],
            })
            return

        # Parse and forward the evaluator's JSON response
        try:
            result = json.loads(proc.stdout)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            self._send_json(200, {
                "score": 0.0,
                "error": "Evaluator output is not valid JSON",
                "stdout": proc.stdout.strip()[:500],
            })
            return

        self._send_json(200, result)

    def _send_json(self, status: int, data: dict) -> None:
        response = json.dumps(data)
        self.send_response(status)
        self.send_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
        self.send_header("Content-Length", str(len(response)))
        self.end_headers()
        self.wfile.write(response.encode())

    def log_message(self, format: str, *args) -> None:
        """Log to stderr with compact format."""
        print(f"[evaluator-http] {args[0]}", file=sys.stderr)


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="evaluator-http-server",
        description="HTTP server wrapping a command evaluator",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8234, help="Port to listen on")
    # Must be last due to nargs="+"
    parser.add_argument(
        "--evaluator-command",
        nargs="+",
        required=True,
        help="Command evaluator to wrap (e.g., bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh)",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    EvaluatorHandler.evaluator_command = args.evaluator_command

    server = HTTPServer(("localhost", args.port), EvaluatorHandler)
    print(f"Listening on http://localhost:{args.port}", file=sys.stderr)
    try:
        server.serve_forever()
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        print("\nShutting down.", file=sys.stderr)
    server.server_close()
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
```

### After writing the file

1. Write the file to `scripts/evaluator_http_server.py`
2. Run the existing test suite to verify nothing is broken: `uv run pytest --tb=short -q`
3. Commit: `git add scripts/evaluator_http_server.py && git commit -m "feat: add HTTP evaluator server wrapping command evaluators"`

Do NOT modify any other files. Do NOT add tests for this file (testing happens live in Task 3).

Task 1 is complete. Here is a summary of what was done:

**File created:** `/Users/aragab/projects/optimize-anything/scripts/evaluator_http_server.py`

**What it does:** A minimal stdlib-only HTTP server that wraps any command evaluator behind an HTTP endpoint. This bridges the gap between `--evaluator-command` scripts (like `evaluators/skill_clarity.sh`) and the CLI's `--evaluator-url` flag.

**Key features:**
- **POST /** — accepts `{"candidate": "..."}` JSON, pipes it to the command evaluator via stdin, returns the evaluator's JSON stdout response
- **GET /** — returns `{"status": "ok"}` for health/preflight checks
- **CLI args:** `--port` (default 8234) and `--evaluator-command` (nargs="+", required, must be last arg)
- **Error handling:** subprocess failures (timeout, not found, non-zero exit, invalid JSON output) all return `{"score": 0.0, "error": "..."}` with HTTP 200, matching the evaluator protocol
- **Startup signal:** prints `Listening on http://localhost:{port}` to stderr
- **Security:** binds to localhost only
- **No external dependencies:** uses only `http.server`, `subprocess`, `argparse`, `json`
- **Compact logging:** `[evaluator-http]` prefix on stderr
- **Clean shutdown:** handles `KeyboardInterrupt`

**Verification:**
- All 222 existing tests pass
- Script imports and parses cleanly
- Committed as `4b6864d`

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""Thin HTTP server wrapping a command evaluator.
     3→
     4→Accepts POST / with {"candidate": "..."}, pipes to the command evaluator
     5→via stdin, and returns the evaluator's JSON response.
     6→
     7→GET / returns {"status": "ok"} for health/preflight checks.
     8→
     9→Usage:
    10→    python scripts/evaluator_http_server.py --evaluator-command bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh
    11→    python scripts/evaluator_http_server.py --port 9000 --evaluator-command bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh
    12→"""
    13→from __future__ import annotations
    14→
    15→import argparse
    16→import json
    17→import subprocess
    18→import sys
    19→from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler
    20→
    21→
    22→class EvaluatorHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    23→    """HTTP handler that delegates scoring to a command evaluator."""
    24→
    25→    evaluator_command: list[str] = []
    26→
    27→    def do_GET(self) -> None:
    28→        """Health check endpoint."""
    29→        self._send_json(200, {"status": "ok"})
    30→
    31→    def do_POST(self) -> None:
    32→        """Score a candidate via the command evaluator."""
    33→        content_length = int(self.headers.get("Content-Length", 0))
    34→        if content_length == 0:
    35→            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Empty request body"})
    36→            return
    37→
    38→        body = self.rfile.read(content_length)
    39→        try:
    40→            payload = json.loads(body)
    41→        except json.JSONDecodeError:
    42→            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Invalid JSON in request body"})
    43→            return
    44→
    45→        if "candidate" not in payload:
    46→            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Missing 'candidate' field"})
    47→            return
    48→
    49→        # Forward to command evaluator
    50→        stdin_data = json.dumps({"candidate": payload["candidate"]})
    51→        try:
    52→            proc = subprocess.run(
    53→                self.evaluator_command,
    54→                input=stdin_data,
    55→                capture_output=True,
    56→                text=True,
    57→                timeout=60,
    58→            )
    59→        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
    60→            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Evaluator command timed out"})
    61→            return
    62→        except FileNotFoundError:
    63→            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": f"Command not found: {self.evaluator_command[0]}"})
    64→            return
    65→        except OSError as e:
    66→            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": f"Command failed: {e}"})
    67→            return
    68→
    69→        if proc.returncode != 0:
    70→            self._send_json(200, {
    71→                "score": 0.0,
    72→                "error": f"Evaluator exited with code {proc.returncode}",
    73→                "stderr": proc.stderr.strip()[:500],
    74→            })
    75→            return
    76→
    77→        # Parse and forward the evaluator's JSON response
    78→        try:
    79→            result = json.loads(proc.stdout)
    80→        except json.JSONDecodeError:
    81→            self._send_json(200, {
    82→                "score": 0.0,
    83→                "error": "Evaluator output is not valid JSON",
    84→                "stdout": proc.stdout.strip()[:500],
    85→            })
    86→            return
    87→
    88→        self._send_json(200, result)
    89→
    90→    def _send_json(self, status: int, data: dict) -> None:
    91→        response = json.dumps(data)
    92→        self.send_response(status)
    93→        self.send_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
    94→        self.send_header("Content-Length", str(len(response)))
    95→        self.end_headers()
    96→        self.wfile.write(response.encode())
    97→
    98→    def log_message(self, format: str, *args) -> None:
    99→        """Log to stderr with compact format."""
   100→        print(f"[evaluator-http] {args[0]}", file=sys.stderr)
   101→

   105→        prog="evaluator-http-server",

   107→    )

   115→    )

## Spec (from the plan)

Create `scripts/evaluator_http_server.py`:

1. **POST /** — accepts JSON `{"candidate": "..."}`, pipes to command evaluator via stdin, returns evaluator's JSON response
2. **GET /** — returns `{"status": "ok"}` for health/preflight checks
3. **CLI args**: `--port` (default 8234), `--evaluator-command` (nargs="+", required)

9. **Clean shutdown** on KeyboardInterrupt

## Your Review

Read `scripts/evaluator_http_server.py` and check:

1. Does every spec requirement have a corresponding implementation?
2. Is there anything implemented that wasn't in the spec? (over-engineering)
3. Are there any edge cases the spec requires that are missing?

content.text:
Here is the compliance review:

---

## Spec Compliance Review: `scripts/evaluator_http_server.py`

**Requirement 1: POST / — accepts JSON `{"candidate": "..."}`, pipes to command evaluator via stdin, returns evaluator's JSON response**

PASS. Lines 31-88 implement this exactly. The handler reads the body, extracts `candidate`, serializes it back to `{"candidate": ...}` for stdin (line 50), runs the subprocess (lines 52-58), parses stdout as JSON, and returns it with `_send_json(200, result)` (line 88).

**Requirement 2: GET / — returns `{"status": "ok"}` for health/preflight checks**

PASS. Lines 27-29 implement `do_GET` returning exactly `{"status": "ok"}`.

**Requirement 3: CLI args — `--port` (default 8234), `--evaluator-command` (nargs="+", required)**

PASS. Lines 108-115 declare both. `--port` defaults to 8234. `--evaluator-command` uses `nargs="+"` and `required=True`.

**Requirement 4: Error handling — subprocess failures return `{"score": 0.0, "error": "..."}` with HTTP 200**

PASS. Multiple failure paths all use `_send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": ...})`:
- Timeout: line 60
- FileNotFoundError: line 63
- OSError: line 66
- Non-zero returncode: lines 69-75
- Invalid JSON output: lines 80-86

**Requirement 5: Startup signal — prints `Listening on http://localhost:{port}` to stderr**

PASS. Line 121 prints exactly `f"Listening on http://localhost:{args.port}"` to `sys.stderr`.

**Requirement 6: Bind to localhost only**

PASS. Line 120: `HTTPServer(("localhost", args.port), ...)` — binds to `"localhost"`, not `"0.0.0.0"`.

**Requirement 7: No external dependencies — stdlib only**

PASS. Imports on lines 15-19: `argparse`, `json`, `subprocess`, `sys`, `http.server` — all stdlib. No third-party packages.

**Requirement 8: Compact logging to stderr with `[evaluator-http]` prefix**

PASS. Lines 98-100 override `log_message` and print to `sys.stderr` with the `[evaluator-http]` prefix.

**Requirement 9: Clean shutdown on KeyboardInterrupt**

PASS. Lines 122-127: `KeyboardInterrupt` is caught, a shutdown message is printed, and `server.server_close()` is called to release the socket.

---

### MISSING items

None.

### EXTRA items (not in spec)

The following are present in the implementation but not mentioned in the spec. They are all defensive input validation, not over-engineering of new features:

1. **Empty body guard** (lines 34-36): Returns `{"score": 0.0, "error": "Empty request body"}` with HTTP 400 when `Content-Length` is 0.
2. **Invalid JSON body guard** (lines 40-43): Returns 400 on malformed request JSON.
3. **Missing `candidate` field guard** (lines 45-47): Returns 400 if the `candidate` key is absent.
4. **`stderr` field in non-zero returncode error** (line 73): Includes `"stderr"` key in the failure response for diagnostics.
5. **`stdout` field in invalid JSON output error** (line 84): Includes `"stdout"` key for diagnostics.
6. **60-second subprocess timeout** (line 57): Hardcoded; spec does not mention a timeout value.

These extras are all conservative, defensive behaviors. The 400 responses for malformed input are arguably debatable against the spec (which says subprocess failures return 200), but they apply to request-level errors before the subprocess is even invoked, so they do not conflict with the spec requirement.

---

### Final Verdict: PASS

All 9 spec requirements are implemented correctly. The extra items are all input validation guards and diagnostic fields that do not contradict the spec — they handle cases the spec simply does not enumerate. No spec requirement is missing.

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""Thin HTTP server wrapping a command evaluator.
     3→
     4→Accepts POST / with {"candidate": "..."}, pipes to the command evaluator
     5→via stdin, and returns the evaluator's JSON response.
     6→
     7→GET / returns {"status": "ok"} for health/preflight checks.
     8→
     9→Usage:
    10→    python scripts/evaluator_http_server.py --evaluator-command bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh
    11→    python scripts/evaluator_http_server.py --port 9000 --evaluator-command bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh
    12→"""
    13→from __future__ import annotations
    14→
    15→import argparse
    16→import json
    17→import subprocess
    18→import sys
    19→from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler
    20→
    21→
    22→class EvaluatorHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    23→    """HTTP handler that delegates scoring to a command evaluator."""
    24→
    25→    evaluator_command: list[str] = []
    26→
    27→    def do_GET(self) -> None:
    28→        """Health check endpoint."""
    29→        self._send_json(200, {"status": "ok"})
    30→
    31→    def do_POST(self) -> None:
    32→        """Score a candidate via the command evaluator."""
    33→        content_length = int(self.headers.get("Content-Length", 0))
    34→        if content_length == 0:
    35→            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Empty request body"})
    36→            return
    37→
    38→        body = self.rfile.read(content_length)
    39→        try:
    40→            payload = json.loads(body)
    41→        except json.JSONDecodeError:
    42→            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Invalid JSON in request body"})
    43→            return
    44→
    45→        if "candidate" not in payload:
    46→            self._send_json(400, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Missing 'candidate' field"})
    47→            return
    48→
    49→        # Forward to command evaluator
    50→        stdin_data = json.dumps({"candidate": payload["candidate"]})
    51→        try:
    52→            proc = subprocess.run(
    53→                self.evaluator_command,
    54→                input=stdin_data,
    55→                capture_output=True,
    56→                text=True,
    57→                timeout=60,
    58→            )
    59→        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
    60→            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": "Evaluator command timed out"})
    61→            return
    62→        except FileNotFoundError:
    63→            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": f"Command not found: {self.evaluator_command[0]}"})
    64→            return
    65→        except OSError as e:
    66→            self._send_json(200, {"score": 0.0, "error": f"Command failed: {e}"})
    67→            return
    68→
    69→        if proc.returncode != 0:
    70→            self._send_json(200, {
    71→                "score": 0.0,
    72→                "error": f"Evaluator exited with code {proc.returncode}",
    73→                "stderr": proc.stderr.strip()[:500],
    74→            })
    75→            return
    76→
    77→        # Parse and forward the evaluator's JSON response
    78→        try:
    79→            result = json.loads(proc.stdout)
    80→        except json.JSONDecodeError:
    81→            self._send_json(200, {
    82→                "score": 0.0,
    83→                "error": "Evaluator output is not valid JSON",
    84→                "stdout": proc.stdout.strip()[:500],
    85→            })
    86→            return
    87→
    88→        self._send_json(200, result)
    89→
    90→    def _send_json(self, status: int, data: dict) -> None:
    91→        response = json.dumps(data)
    92→        self.send_response(status)
    93→        self.send_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
    94→        self.send_header("Content-Length", str(len(response)))
    95→        self.end_headers()
    96→        self.wfile.write(response.encode())
    97→
    98→    def log_message(self, format: str, *args) -> None:
    99→        """Log to stderr with compact format."""
   100→        print(f"[evaluator-http] {args[0]}", file=sys.stderr)
   101→
   102→
   103→def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
   104→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   105→        prog="evaluator-http-server",
   106→        description="HTTP server wrapping a command evaluator",
   107→    )
   108→    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8234, help="Port to listen on")
   109→    # Must be last due to nargs="+"
   110→    parser.add_argument(
   111→        "--evaluator-command",
   112→        nargs="+",
   113→        required=True,
   114→        help="Command evaluator to wrap (e.g., bash evaluators/skill_clarity.sh)",
   115→    )
   116→    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
   117→
   118→    EvaluatorHandler.evaluator_command = args.evaluator_command
   119→
   120→    server = HTTPServer(("localhost", args.port), EvaluatorHandler)
   121→    print(f"Listening on http://localhost:{args.port}", file=sys.stderr)
   122→    try:
   123→        server.serve_forever()
   124→    except KeyboardInterrupt:
   125→        print("\nShutting down.", file=sys.stderr)
   126→    server.server_close()
   127→    return 0
   128→
   129→
   130→if __name__ == "__main__":
   131→    sys.exit(main())
   132→

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""Regression gate: verify artifact scores have not dropped below baseline.
     3→
     4→Reads a scores.json file, runs each evaluator against its artifact, and
     5→exits non-zero if any score falls below the recorded baseline (with a
     6→small tolerance).
     7→
     8→Usage:
     9→    python scripts/score_check.py [--scores scores.json] [--root .]
    10→"""
    11→
    12→from __future__ import annotations
    13→
    14→import argparse
    15→import json
    16→import subprocess
    17→import sys
    18→from pathlib import Path
    19→
    20→TOLERANCE = 0.01
    21→
    22→
    23→def load_scores(scores_path: Path) -> dict:
    24→    """Read and parse the scores.json file."""
    25→    with open(scores_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    26→        return json.load(f)
    27→
    28→
    29→def read_artifact(artifact_path: Path) -> str:
    30→    """Read an artifact file and return its text content."""
    31→    return artifact_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    32→
    33→
    34→def run_evaluator(evaluator_path: Path, candidate: str, cwd: Path) -> float:
    35→    """Pipe a candidate through an evaluator and return the score.
    36→
    37→    The evaluator receives JSON on stdin: {"candidate": "<text>"}
    38→    and must output JSON on stdout with a numeric "score" field.
    39→    """
    40→    payload = json.dumps({"candidate": candidate})
    41→    try:
    42→        proc = subprocess.run(
    43→            ["bash", str(evaluator_path)],
    44→            input=payload,
    45→            capture_output=True,
    46→            text=True,
    47→            cwd=str(cwd),
    48→            timeout=30,
    49→        )
    50→    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired as exc:
    51→        raise RuntimeError(
    52→            f"Evaluator {evaluator_path} timed out after 30s"
    53→        ) from exc
    54→    if proc.returncode != 0:
    55→        raise RuntimeError(
    56→            f"Evaluator {evaluator_path} exited with code {proc.returncode}: "
    57→            f"{proc.stderr.strip()}"
    58→        )
    59→    result = json.loads(proc.stdout)
    60→    score = result["score"]
    61→    if not isinstance(score, (int, float)):
    62→        raise ValueError(f"Evaluator returned non-numeric score: {score!r}")
    63→    return float(score)
    64→
    65→
    66→def check_scores(
    67→    scores_path: Path,
    68→    root: Path,
    69→    *,
    70→    update: bool = False,
    71→    run_id: str | None = None,
    72→) -> int:
    73→    """Run all evaluators and compare scores to baselines.
    74→
    75→    Returns 0 if all artifacts pass, 1 if any fail.
    76→    When update=True and all checks pass, writes measured scores back as new baselines
    77→    and appends a history entry.
    78→    """
    79→    scores = load_scores(scores_path)
    80→    any_failed = False
    81→    measured: dict[str, float] = {}
    82→
    83→    for artifact_rel, entry in scores.items():
    84→        artifact_path = root / artifact_rel
    85→        evaluator_rel = entry["evaluator"]
    86→        evaluator_path = root / evaluator_rel
    87→        baseline = entry["baseline"]
    88→
    89→        # Check artifact exists
    90→        if not artifact_path.exists():
    91→            print(f"FAIL  {artifact_rel}  (artifact not found: {artifact_path})")
    92→            any_failed = True
    93→            continue
    94→
    95→        # Check evaluator exists
    96→        if not evaluator_path.exists():
    97→            print(f"FAIL  {artifact_rel}  (evaluator not found: {evaluator_path})")
    98→            any_failed = True
    99→            continue
   100→
   101→        try:
   102→            candidate = read_artifact(artifact_path)
   103→            score = run_evaluator(evaluator_path, candidate, cwd=root)
   104→        except Exception as exc:
   105→            print(f"FAIL  {artifact_rel}  (error: {exc})")
   106→            any_failed = True
   107→            continue
   108→
   109→        measured[artifact_rel] = score
   110→
   111→        if score >= baseline - TOLERANCE:
   112→            print(f"PASS  {artifact_rel}  score={score:.4f}  baseline={baseline:.4f}")
   113→        else:
   114→            print(
   115→                f"FAIL  {artifact_rel}  score={score:.4f}  baseline={baseline:.4f}  "
   116→                f"(dropped by {baseline - score:.4f})"
   117→            )
   118→            any_failed = True
   119→
   120→    if not any_failed and update:
   121→        _write_back_scores(scores_path, scores, measured, run_id=run_id)
   122→        print(f"Updated baselines written to {scores_path}")
   123→
   124→    return 1 if any_failed else 0
   125→
   126→
   127→def _write_back_scores(
   128→    scores_path: Path,
   129→    original: dict,
   130→    measured: dict[str, float],
   131→    *,
   132→    run_id: str | None = None,
   133→) -> None:
   134→    """Write measured scores back to scores.json as new baselines and append history."""
   135→    from datetime import date, datetime, timezone
   136→
   137→    today = date.today().isoformat()
   138→    updated = {}
   139→    for artifact_rel, entry in original.items():
   140→        new_entry = dict(entry)
   141→        if artifact_rel in measured:
   142→            score = round(measured[artifact_rel], 6)
   143→            new_entry["baseline"] = score
   144→            new_entry["current"] = score
   145→            new_entry["last_run"] = today
   146→            history = list(new_entry.get("history", []))
   147→            history.append({
   148→                "score": score,
   149→                "date": today,
   150→                "run_id": run_id,
   151→            })
   152→            new_entry["history"] = history
   153→        updated[artifact_rel] = new_entry
   154→
   155→    with open(scores_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   156→        json.dump(updated, f, indent=2)
   157→        f.write("\n")
   158→
   159→
   160→def parse_args(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> argparse.Namespace:
   161→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   162→        description="Regression gate: check artifact scores against baselines."
   163→    )
   164→    parser.add_argument(
   165→        "--scores",
   166→        default="scores.json",
   167→        help="Path to scores.json (default: scores.json)",
   168→    )
   169→    parser.add_argument(
   170→        "--root",
   171→        default=".",
   172→        help="Root directory for resolving artifact and evaluator paths (default: .)",
   173→    )
   174→    parser.add_argument(
   175→        "--update",
   176→        action="store_true",
   177→        default=False,
   178→        help=(
   179→            "After all checks pass, write measured scores back to scores.json "
   180→            "as new baselines and append to history. Does not update if any check fails."
   181→        ),
   182→    )
   183→    parser.add_argument(
   184→        "--run-id",
   185→        default=None,
   186→        help="Optional run identifier (e.g. git SHA) to record in history.",
   187→    )
   188→    return parser.parse_args(argv)
   189→
   190→
   191→def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
   192→    args = parse_args(argv)
   193→    scores_path = Path(args.scores)
   194→    root = Path(args.root)
   195→
   196→    if not scores_path.exists():
   197→        print(f"Error: scores file not found: {scores_path}", file=sys.stderr)
   198→        return 1
   199→
   200→    return check_scores(scores_path, root, update=args.update, run_id=args.run_id)
   201→
   202→
   203→if __name__ == "__main__":
   204→    raise SystemExit(main())
   205→
